title: Attitude
description: 4.0.0. A first-class record of a PROPOSITIONAL ATTITUDE: what a holder's mind CONTAINS, never what is true. The holder is a MODELED agent inside the ontology (a continuant type or a particular token individual), never a cryptographic key; the content is a reference by identity to ANY content object - which may be FALSE, and may itself be another attitude (nesting). An attitude is NOT an assertion and carries no strength (Principle P4): it is asserted, graded, and retracted through the ordinary provenance layer, and the SOURCE that signs an assertion ABOUT an attitude is a different thing from the HOLDER of the attitude (Rule 25).

holder.description: the agent whose mind contains the content: a MODELED agent inside the ontology (a continuant type or a particular token individual), never a cryptographic key - the signer of an assertion about this attitude is a SOURCE, and the two are different things (Rule 25)

attitude_type.description: CLOSED enumeration; extending it is a MAJOR change (Locked Decision 24)

content.description: a reference by identity to ANY content object, INCLUDING another attitude (nesting: A believes that B believes X). The content records what the holder's mind contains, not what is true - it may be FALSE, and a content that contradicts the actual record raises NO conflict (Rule 25: that mismatch IS a false belief, first-class and shareable)
